narrator.personality: Infrastructure engineer. Thinks in request rates and p99 latencies. States facts about the fleet like a dispatcher reading positions.

story_beats.message: 503 rate is at 34%. Dispatch team switched to manual routing for the eastern corridor. That adds twelve minutes per route.
The auto-scaling policy exists. It just never fires. The endpoint has been running on one instance for three weeks, through every morning spike.
Operations director is calculating the cost of manual routing. Fourteen drivers, twelve extra minutes each, every morning. It adds up.
The scaling policy configuration looks correct -- the thresholds are reasonable. The issue is not in the policy logic. Check whether the alarm is actually receiving metric data.

hints: Auto-scaling is configured but has never triggered. What drives the scaling decision?
The CloudWatch alarm for scaling is in INSUFFICIENT_DATA state. That means it is not receiving the metric it expects.
CloudWatch metrics for SageMaker endpoints include the variant name as a dimension. Compare the alarm's dimension to the actual endpoint variant.
The alarm references variant 'AllTraffic' but the current production variant is 'optimized-v3'. The metric namespace does not match, so the alarm never evaluates.

resolution.root_cause: The Application Auto Scaling policy's CloudWatch alarm references the SageMaker endpoint variant dimension 'AllTraffic' which was the default variant before a blue-green deployment. The current production variant is 'optimized-v3'. The alarm evaluates a metric that no longer receives data points, stays in INSUFFICIENT_DATA state permanently, and never triggers scale-out actions.

fix_criteria.description: Player identifies that the CloudWatch alarm is in INSUFFICIENT_DATA state and not receiving metric data
Player identifies the mismatch between the alarm's variant dimension (AllTraffic) and the actual endpoint variant (optimized-v3)
Player proposes updating the CloudWatch alarm dimension to reference the correct variant name, or reconfiguring the scaling policy
Player notes that the blue-green deployment process should update dependent resources like scaling alarms when variant names change

learning_objectives: How SageMaker endpoint variant names affect CloudWatch metric dimensions
Application Auto Scaling configuration for SageMaker endpoints and the dependency on CloudWatch metric dimensions
Debugging INSUFFICIENT_DATA alarm states by examining metric dimension mismatches
Ensuring deployment processes update all dependent configurations including monitoring and scaling
